notes: Equality and identity semantics plus required runtime conformance IDs are machine-validated
Class/object init ordering and closed-shape field initialization are enforced statically
Closed-shape member access is validated for self and nominal objects with method/field call-shape checks
Catch-all fallback required for V1 match expressions; duplicate literal arms are compile-time errors.
V1 async/coroutine/generator scope is machine-enforced as out-of-profile with deterministic diagnostics
ABI/extern boundary is explicitly contract-validated with deterministic fallback behavior
Inline module dependencies are topologically ordered and cycles are compile-time errors
Deterministic source lockfile generation and validation are CI-enforced
Exception-channel panic transport and try/cleanup/reraise behavior are contract-validated in CI
Single-thread semantic domain is validated and async/task primitives are explicitly rejected
Mutex/atomic/channel/taskgroup vocabulary is explicitly out-of-profile and CI-validated
V1 memory-ordering semantics are explicitly none and enforced by policy validation
Structured cancellation is explicitly out-of-profile in V1 with deterministic validation
Cross-backend memory strategy invariants are machine-validated (GC + RC parity mode)
Unsafe surface is prohibited by policy and validated against runtime/backends
FFI lifecycle and extern fallback behavior are contract-validated with conformance anchors
jenc/jdec builtins implemented with runtime conformance and semantic type checks
Closed-shape objects + typed self/nominal dispatch + method capability composition are regression-locked
Default-field type propagation is validated for downstream method typing
Collections/strings/time helpers are runtime-backed and regression-covered (including datetime/tz and conversion helpers)
Literal zero-div and literal index OOB are caught statically; runtime enforces bounds and missing-member safety
Static transitive capability composition and runtime allow-list enforcement are implemented and gated
Version pinning and deprecation autofix migration are validated in CI
Interop contract is machine-validated for ABI embedding WASM and JSON builtin parity
